methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $141M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
MacroFab, a Series C robotics and manufacturing platform founded by Chris Church, has raised $141M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: 1 public and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and Hadrian.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $141M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $240M. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company set—with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B—points to a median of $1.6B, anchored by comparable Series C and adjacent-stage firms. The peer set includes Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C) and DexteTORT at $2.0B (Series D), establishing the upper-quartile benchmark.

The triangulated valuation range spans $204M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $920M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether MacroFab's capital efficiency, market traction, and stage readiness align with the lower end of the peer distribution or closer to Hadrian and other scaling robotics platforms. Capital raised alone underweights sector momentum; peer comparables anchor the higher end. Key variables include revenue scale, gross margins, and path to Series D readiness.
